name: Cherry Street Brewing
quip: The original Forsyth County brewery — the first ones legally allowed to whip up craft beer in the area.
review:
🐾🎉 Hey there, my fabulous fur-friends! Let me tell you about my latest adventure at Cherry Street Brewery in Vickery Village! Now, let me drop some knowledge on you: they're the original Forsyth County brewery, the first ones legally allowed to whip up craft beer in the area. Talk about a paw-some legacy! 🍻✨

Not only do they serve up some seriously good brews and tasty bites, but this place is also super dog-friendly! They really roll out the red carpet for us pups, especially for Frenchies like yours truly. I made a whole pack of friends, including our beertenders, Leah and Zach. Leah was a total gem—every time she came in and out, I got a fresh round of pets! I mean, can you blame her? Who wouldn't want to pet this adorable face? 😍🐶

Now, let's talk about the food! They've got these things called chicken lips, and let me tell you, they were lip-smackin' good! I was begging for a taste, but my humans insisted on buffalo style. Rude, right? No chicken for me! But they did have treats, so I guess I'll let it slide this time. I mean, who can stay mad when there are snacks involved? 🍗🐾

Now, onto the important stuff—THE BEER! My humans went all out and tasted everything Cherry Street had to offer. For their go-to style, the IPAs, they tried the West LA Hopaway, Soul Rebel Hazy, and Les Brers. All of these got well over a 3 on Untappd, which is like a gold star in the beer world! But the real winner of the day? Drumroll, please… the Black Throated Stout – Sour Blackberry! It was unexpectedly fantastic, and my mom was practically begging for seconds. I mean, who knew beer could be so fancy? 🍺🍇

Cherry Street has a super cool bar area and a large outdoor seating area, perfect for soaking up that beautiful Atlanta spring weather. Plus, it's in a prime spot for me to send some peemail to all my doggy pals. If you're in the Vickery area, you've got to check this place out! Trust me, you won't regret it! 🐾❤️ Cheers to good times, great brews, and even better friends! 🍻✨

name: Crooked Culture Brewing
quip: This should be more but lots of unleashed children!
review:
Woof woof, fellow fur-friends and human companions! It's your favorite brew-hopping Frenchie, Palmer, here to share my thoughts on a pawsitively delightful spot: Crooked Culture Brewery in Cumming, GA! Now, let's get one thing straight—when it comes to brewery ratings, it's all about location, location, location! And Crooked Culture is nestled in the primo City Center, making it a tail-wagging destination for both humans and their four-legged pals.

First off, let's talk about the humans. While they rave about the fantastic beers (and trust me, we'll get to that), I can't help but notice all the shopping options nearby! My humans love to stroll through Top Knotch Boutique, Pieces and Peaches, and Rugged and Refined. The best part? The lovely ladies and gents at these shops are always ready to give me a belly rub and a tasty treat! It's like a treasure hunt for my humans, and I get to enjoy the spoils!

Now, onto the main event—Crooked Culture Brewery! As soon as you step inside, you're greeted by a cozy "living room" vibe, complete with large leather sofas and a big TV to catch our favorite games. It's the perfect spot for my humans to kick back and relax while I soak up all the attention. And let's not forget about the food trucks that often park outside, serving up delicious bites to complement those frosty brews!

Speaking of brews, my humans have a soft spot for IPAs (like, duh, who doesn't?), and Crooked Culture has some real winners on tap. Two that really stand out are "Sounds and Gestures" and "This One Slaps." They're always raving about how hoppy and refreshing they are! But here's the kicker—my humans are also fans of the Santa's Slay Ride Imperial Stout. They say it's like Christmas in a mug, and I can't argue with that! If only they'd share a sip with me (just kidding, I'll stick to my treats)!

In conclusion, if you're looking for a paw-some brewery experience with great beer, a comfy atmosphere, and plenty of shopping and dining options within walking distance, Crooked Culture Brewery is the place to be! So grab your humans, head on over, and let's raise a glass (or a bowl) to good times and great brews! Cheers! 🐾🐾

name: Frog Rock Brewing Company
quip: They brew "Happy Beer" with lithium-rich mineral water.
review:
At the recommendation of my buddy Justin at Skint Chestnut, we hopped over to Austell, GA, to meet Justice and his dog, Miss Moo Moo. Now, I know she has a fancy name that's probably longer than my leash, but let's be real—Miss Moo Moo is way more fun to say!

Talk about dog-friendly! We rolled in, and this girl was ready to play. We were instant besties—#BFFs for life! Sundays are our special day because that's when my friend is guaranteed to be there, and I can't resist a good game of chase. Frog Rock may not be huge, but they have a super cool outdoor space in the back that's perfect for sniffing all the smells and leaving little messages for my fellow canine visitors. It's like my own personal doggy playground!

Justice is just as cool as Justin, and he knows the people and their tastes better than I know where my humans hide the treats. The humans rave about their beers, but I'm just here for the popcorn—my ultimate weakness! They don't have dog treats per se, but if you find a tiny human, you're bound to score some of that buttery goodness. Just make sure to give them your best puppy eyes; it's a foolproof strategy!

The vibe at Frog Rock is also super chill. Not a single visit has gone by without my humans and I making new friends at the bar. Sometimes, we even take them along on our next adventure—because who doesn't want to hang out with a French bulldog on a mission?

Now, here's the kicker: the unique vision of Head Brewer and Co-founder Ryan. He's brewing what he calls "Happy Beer." Why? Because their water comes from Lithia Springs Mineral Water, which contains trace amounts of Lithium. Yes, you heard that right! So, as you sip on a Frog Rock beverage, you might find your spirits soaring higher than a squirrel in a tree! Alcohol is usually a natural depressant, but thanks to the Lithium in your beer, you might just feel like you can conquer the world—or at least the nearest dog park!

So, if you're into a cool vibe, good beer, and a unique vision that might just make you feel like a million bucks (or at least like you've had a few too many belly rubs), go check out Frog Rock Brewery! Cheers to good brews, great friends, and the best popcorn around! 🍻🐾

name: Gate City Brewing Company
quip: Frenchie-approved? Double check! 🐾🍻
review:
It was a paws-itively toasty day in Atlanta when we trotted over to Gate City Brewing for the first time, and let me tell you—all of Atlanta and their dogs had the same idea! Located in the heart of Roswell, GA, this spot is prime territory for pups and their humans to sip, stroll, and socialize, thanks to that sweet, sweet open-container law.

Now, let's talk drinks—they've got the goods! My humans were thrilled to find not just a stellar beer lineup but also some top-tier craft cocktails for those who like to mix things up. The space? Absolutely massive! Perfect for zoomies, impromptu wrestling matches, and some good ol' fashioned butt-sniffing galore. Whether you're a sprinter or a sniffer, there's plenty of room to get your social on.

Speaking of VIP treatment, when we bellied up to the bar, the beertender was johnny on the spot with a treat just for me—now that's how you win over a discerning beer-hopping Frenchie! And talk about a dog party—Frenchies were out in full force. I made so many new friends, but Louie, buddy, you take the cake! Let's schedule a treat-and-greet soon, okay?

As for my humans, they gave high marks to Awe Juice, Pebbles, and a Bourbon cocktail that hit all the right notes. I didn't pay much attention—I was far too busy making friends, zooming across the patio, and perfecting my brewery ambassador duties.

If you find yourself in the East Cobb area, put Gate City on your must-visit list. Treats? Check. Good vibes? Check. Space for zoomies and butt-sniffing? Oh, you bet! Frenchie-approved? Double check! 🐾🍻

name: Green Line Brewery
quip: This place is a bit of a hole in the wall, but it's got more vibe than a dog park on a sunny day!
review:
🐾🎉 Let me tell you about my latest adventure at a hidden gem in downtown Canton, GA—Green Line Brewery! This place is a bit of a hole in the wall, but it's got more vibe than a dog park on a sunny day! 🌞🐶

The atmosphere at Green Line is so welcoming that it's like one big happy family reunion every weekend! Seriously, people keep coming back for six years—talk about loyalty! And while there's a TV behind the bar, unless the Dawgs are playing, it's not always on. Why? Because everyone is too busy soaking in the good times, sipping on tasty craft beer, and jamming out to amazing live music from local artists. I mean, who needs TV when you've got live tunes and a bunch of humans who love dogs? 🎶🍻

Now, let's talk about the walls. You might find a patron or two taking a marker to the featured plastered wall, which used to be white but is now a colorful masterpiece! It's filled with phrases, conspiracy theories, initials, doodles, and songs from all the guests who've visited. It's like a giant scrapbook of memories! And guess what? They have a massive sticker wall, and you know I had to add my Frenchie flair to it! Now they're official Frenchies on Tap fans! 🎨🐾

The owners are super friendly and always out on the floor getting to know everyone's stories. It's more like a niche community than your average brewpub. We met one of the owners, Joel, and man was he an awesome guy, who even gave us his phone number to chat about a collaboration to benefit SHARE! How cool is that? I also got to meet some veterans, hand out my stat cards, and spread the word about our program. I'm basically a doggy ambassador now! 🇺🇸🐕

Now, let's get to the important stuff—THE BEER! We tried their house Shirtless IPA, and let me tell you, it was tastier than a fresh bowl of kibble! If we didn't have more stops on our adventure, we would've definitely tried a few more. And rumor has it they have exceptional tacos, so you know we'll be back to chat it up with Joel and the patrons while munching on some tasty bites and sipping on more brews! 🌮🍺

So if you're in the Canton area, make sure to check out Green Line Brewery! It's a paw-some place to hang out, make new friends, and enjoy some tail-wagging good times! Cheers to good vibes and even better brews! 🍻🐾✨

name: Horned Owl Brewing
quip: Over the last year, Horned Owl Brewing has quickly become MY favorite brewery close to home.
review:
Hey there, hoomans! It's me, Palmer, your favorite beer-loving French bulldog and self-proclaimed connoisseur of all things tasty! Over the last year, Horned Owl Brewing (HOB) has quickly become MY favorite brewery close to home. I mean, it's got all the five paws in all the categories—yes, I'm counting my paws, and yes, I'm a little biased, but who wouldn't be when you're this fabulous?

Let's start with the most important topic: BEER! The beer at HOB gets all the paws, and I'm not just barking up the wrong tree here. They're cranking out new brews faster than a labrador can chase its tail, and with about 12-14 beers on tap at any given time, there's something for every human palate. My humans are all about those IPAs. They rave about Poncho (which is zippy with a little jalapeño—perfect for those spicy taco nights!), Snow Owl, Raptor (because who doesn't want to drink like a dinosaur?), and Mango Feathers. If you're looking for something lighter, The Owlet is a great all-day sipper. Trust me, we've verified that is the case—multiple times. Quality control, you know?

Now, let's lump treats, atmosphere, and the staff together because from my doggie perspective, this is where the real magic happens at HOB. The staff gets so many paws up, I'm surprised they don't have a fan club! They're not just dog-friendly; they're dog-obsessed! They remember the humans who come in and even the beers they like. I mean, they know my humans better than I do! And did I mention they're great at checking in to see if you need another beer? They're like my personal butlers, but with less bowing and more belly rubs.

Speaking of belly rubs, I have two personal favorite humans at HOB that I know by name. When my humans say, "Want to go see Preston or Nick?" it's always a resounding YES! I practically leap off the couch like I'm auditioning for the dog Olympics. And let's not forget about Emily and Stacia—those ladies are treat dispensers in disguise! I'm convinced they have a secret stash of goodies just for me.

So, if you're looking for a place that serves up great beer, friendly staff, and treats that make my tail wag like a windmill, Horned Owl Brewery is the place to be! Just remember to bring your best furry friend along—because what's better than sipping a cold one while your dog is living their best life? Cheers to good times, great brews, and endless belly rubs! 🐾🍻

name: Lazy Guy Distillery
quip: Great drinks, a cool vibe, and a pup-friendly atmosphere? What more could a dog ask for?
review:
Hey there, fellow fur-friends and humans! So, my humans and I decided to shake things up a bit and diverge from our usual brewery hopping to check out this place called Lazy Guy Distillery in downtown Kennesaw. Let me tell you, it's located in a super cool building from the 1800s that just oozes character! The patio vibe is pawsitively delightful, and I could tell right away that this was going to be a fun outing.

Now, for all my fellow pups out there, you'll be happy to know that Lazy Guy has thought of us too! They've got these awesome river stones that are perfect for cooling off on a hot day—trust me, I can attest to their effectiveness! Plus, Tracy, our lovely server, was an absolute gem. She brought me an ice-cold water bowl, some tasty treats, and even threw in a belly rub! Talk about a five-paw experience!

As for my humans, they tried two cocktails that had them doing a little happy dance: a pumpkin spice mule and a habanero honey mule. It's clear that the folks at Lazy Guy have some serious experience when it comes to concocting craft cocktails!

Oh, and if you're curious about how they make all this magic happen, they offer tours on a limited basis. Just keep an eye on their Instagram and Facebook for updates! If you take a quick peek in the back, you'll see they've got quite the setup going on—definitely worth a look!

So, if you're in the area and looking for a fun spot to chill with your humans, I highly recommend Lazy Guy Distillery. Great drinks, a cool vibe, and a pup-friendly atmosphere? What more could a dog ask for? Until next time, keep those tails (or butts) wagging! 🐾🍹

name: Mutation Brewing
location: 5825 Roswell Rd NE, Sandy Springs, GA 30328
quip: This place is HUGE! I walked in and felt like a celebrity.
review:
🐾🎉 Hey there, my fabulous fur-friends! I am back at it again with another butt shakin' adventure! This time, we ventured across 285 to Roswell Rd to check out Mutation Brewing. And let me tell you, this place is HUGE! I walked in and felt like a celebrity—everyone loves a Frenchie, am I right? There were tons of fellow Frenchie lovers and owners at their little market, and I was basically the main attraction! Move over, beer; it's all about the Frenchie fame! 🌟🐶

The beer tenders were totally on my wavelength, too. They had a cup of treats ready to go, which is basically the best kind of service. I mean, who needs a fancy cocktail when you can have a treat in every paw? 🍖🍻

Now, this was just a quick stop on our brewery tour, but my humans managed to squeeze in some tastings. They tried the Liquid Hop Magma, Sharks with Laser Beams, and a favorite, Hooked on Phoenix (Book 5). The Hooked on Phoenix was a super crisp, citrusy IPA that had my humans dreaming of the beach. I mean, I'd love to hit the beach too, but I'm more of a "chase the seagulls" kind of girl! 🏖️🐦

Mutation Brewing has a lot going on—there's an event space, a cool outdoor area with fun swing chairs, trivia nights, and the best part? ALL the dogs are invited! It's like a doggy paradise! 🐾🎈

While we didn't get to taste any of the food, they do have a kitchen, so you can get your eat on too. I mean, who wouldn't want to munch on some tasty bites while sipping on delicious brews? So if you're looking for a paw-some place to hang out with your humans and fellow pups, Mutation Brewing is the spot! Trust me, you won't want to miss it! Cheers to good times and even better treats! 🍻🐾✨

name: NoFo Brew Co
location: Cumming, GA (also Cleveland & Gainesville, GA locations)
quip: Knowing life isn't fair, I don't think it's fair that kids can play fetch and I can't BUT I'm always welcome – and they let me on the couch!
review:
Hey there, fellow fur-friends and human companions! It's your favorite brew-hopping Frenchie, Palmer, back with another tail-wagging review! Today, I'm excited to share my thoughts on a pawsitively fantastic spot: NoFo Brew Co! If you're looking for a place that's got it all—great drinks, a cozy atmosphere, and plenty of space for us pups—then this is the place for you!

What I love most about NoFo Brew Co are the comfy sofas that make for the perfect lounging spot after a long day of sniffing and exploring. And let's not forget the gigantic play area! It's like my own personal dog park, where I can run around and show off my best zoomies. Plus, there's a fire pit that's just perfect for those chilly evenings when my humans want to cozy up with a drink while I bask in the warmth. It's a win-win!

Now, let's talk about what my humans enjoy. They're big fans of the craft cocktails here, and I can see why! One of their favorites is the "Big Chili," made with Agave Blanco, fresh citrus, and a pineapple Tajin and salt rim. They also can't get enough of the "Lone Wolf," which combines vodka, jalapeño, peach, pineapple, and lime.

Of course, NoFo Brew Co is also known for its always-available IPA, "Snow Ghost." My humans rave about it, and I can tell it's a crowd-pleaser! But what really makes this place special is the consistent presence of a food truck. That means there are always tasty nibbles to find on the floor—score! And let's be honest, where there are little humans (and there are plenty at NoFo), there are bound to be snacks dropping all around. It's like a treasure hunt for me!

The family vibe at NoFo is another reason we love it so much. It's a place where everyone feels welcome, and I get to meet all sorts of new friends—both human and canine! Whether it's a beautiful day to lounge outside in the large play area or a chilly evening by the bonfire, NoFo Brew Co hits the spot every time.

Oh, and did I mention they have two other locations with equally cool vibes? But we frequent the Cumming spot since it's practically a hop, skip, and a jump away for us.

In conclusion, if you're looking for a paw-some place to enjoy great craft cocktails, delicious food, and a welcoming atmosphere, NoFo Brew Co is the place to be! So grab your humans, head on over, and let's raise a glass (or a bowl) to good times and great brews! Cheers!

name: Pendley Creek Brewing Company
quip: Pendley is all about the purist beers, and they do it right!
review:
So, let me tell you about our find in Jasper, GA! We met Daniel and Pam while property hunting in North Georgia, and now we make it a point to visit Pendley Brewery every few weeks. It's like our little slice of heaven in downtown Jasper, and trust me, it's worth the trip!

Now, if you're looking for jalapeño-infused, fruity concoctions that taste like a smoothie gone wrong, you can keep on searching. Pendley is all about the purist beers, and they do it right! You won't find any weird flavors here—just some seriously awesome traditional brews that make my humans' taste buds do a happy dance. My humans have basically tried the whole menu, and on a scale of 1 to 5, they're all sitting pretty above 3.7, with a few actual 5's thrown in for good measure. So, you can bet your bottom biscuit that you won't be disappointed!

The vibe at Pendley is small and quaint, like a cozy little den where you can kick back and relax. And if you're visiting in the fall, you can bet your favorite chew toy that there's a good football game on the TV. Now, we may have some friendly debates about Daniel's hometown team, but I'll let that slide because he's known to share a chip or two with yours truly. I mean, who can resist a Frenchie?

So, if you're in the area and looking for a place that serves up fantastic beers, a cozy atmosphere, and the occasional snack for your favorite four-legged friend, Pendley Brewery is the place to be! Just remember to give Daniel a little nudge for those chips—he's got a soft spot for me, and I'm not above using my charm to score some tasty treats! Cheers to good brews and even better company! 🍻🐾

name: Reformation Brewery
quip: Why not strut our stuff to downtown Woodstock and hang out with our pals at Reformation Brewery?
review:
🐾🎉 Hey there, here to spill the beans about our latest adventure in the sunny ATL! So, Atlanta's February weather decided to throw a party with a balmy 65 degrees, and my humans and I thought, "Why not strut our stuff to downtown Woodstock and hang out with our pals at Reformation Brewery?" 🌞🍻

Let me tell you, this place has the location game on LOCKDOWN! It's right off the Woodstock trails, smack dab in the heart of downtown, and guess what? They've got an open container law! That means my humans can sip and stroll while I sniff and schmooze. Plus, there's a gigantic outdoor lawn area—perfect for families and pups like me to frolic and make new friends. I even got to meet some fellow Frenchies! It was like a mini Frenchie convention, and I was the guest of honor! 🐶🎈

But before we dive into the beer, I've got to give a shoutout to my fur-tastic friends, Spencer and Paula Nix, the dynamic duo behind Reformation. These two are five stars all the way! Not only have they created an amazing community vibe, but they're also like superheroes for our military and first responders. They're always the first to raise their paws for Shepherd's Men and have raised a ton of dough for the SHARE Military Initiative. Every Memorial Day, they brew a special beer just for Shepherd's Men and throw a massive party in Woodstock. Talk about a butt waggin' good time! 🎉🐾

Now, let's get to the good stuff—BEER! One of the coolest things about Reformation is that they distribute, so you can snag your favorite brews at the grocery store. They've even added some non-alcoholic options and craft cocktails for those humans who aren't as into the hoppy goodness as I am. A couple of brews that are high on our list are A Cold One, Alani (a wine-inspired sour that's as unique as my snout), and the Jude. 🐕💃

And let's not forget the new kid on the block—the Dawn IPA! We were especially excited about the Pride and Prejudice Imperial Stout made in collaboration with Alma Coffee. I mean, who doesn't love a good book reference with their brew? 📚🍺

So, if you're looking for a paw-some place to soak up some rays, meet fellow Frenchies, and enjoy some top-notch brews, Reformation Brewery is where it's at! Trust me, you won't want to miss it! 🐾❤️ Cheers to good times and even better friends! 🍻✨

name: Schoolhouse Brewing
quip: If only this place were a little closer to home, it would definitely be a top contender for my favorite brewery!
review:
Oh la la, fur-friends! If only this place were a little closer to home, it would definitely be a top contender for my favorite brewery! I mean, it could still make the cut, who knows? First off, this place is HUGE! It's like a wonderland for pups and humans alike, with all kinds of awesome spots to explore! 🌟🐶

The vibe here is super cool, and the decorating… Let's just say it's got more character than a squirrel in a tutu! They've got pinball machines, table tennis, and even beer pong for those humans who want to entertain their kids or just relive their college days. 🎱🍻 And don't even get me started on the seating! Old school bus seats, tons of tables and chairs, and an outdoor space that's just begging for a Frenchie sprint! While I'm not supposed to be off-leash, my humans have been known to let me do a few laps when no one's around. I mean, a Frenchie's gotta stretch those legs, right? 🏃‍♂️💨

And speaking of kids, they're like well-behaved little pups themselves! Just kidding! But honestly, for such a big place with so many entertainment options, it's like a schoolhouse for humans and their little ones. I approve! 📚🐾

Now, let's get to the good stuff—BEER! 🍺 I've got to give a shoutout to my favorite beertender, Easa! She really knows how to make a Frenchie feel special. Always ready with a solid recommendation, she's like my personal beer concierge! The great thing about Schoolhouse is they always have at least 15-20 beers on tap, so there's something for everyone! For my humans who are all about those IPAs, they've got plenty of options to keep them happy. Right now, the most exciting brews on the menu are the Full Tilt Boogie, Big Dad Energy, and some Aleuminati. I mean, how can you resist a name like that? 😂✨

And for those non-beer drinkers, they've got some tasty seltzers on tap too! So, whether you're a beer lover or just along for the ride, Schoolhouse has got you covered. Until next time, I'll be dreaming of this place and plotting my next visit! 🐾🍻❤️

name: Skint Chestnut Brewing Company
location: 3886 Broad St Ste 100, Powder Springs, GA 30127
quip: It's like a big, frothy family reunion where everyone's wearing funny shirts and talking about hops instead of politics!
review:
One of the things my humans and I love most about craft beer is the culture surrounding it. It's like a big, frothy family reunion where everyone's wearing funny shirts and talking about hops instead of politics! We get to chat with the brewers, the owners, and the brewtenders to hear their wild stories, what makes their brewery special, and where they go to drink when they're not busy brewing their own magic.

So, one sunny Sunday, we decided to explore an alternative side of town and stumbled upon Skint Chestnut. Let me tell you, it did not disappoint! It's nestled in the charming little downtown area of Powder Springs, GA, where the only thing more delightful than the beer is the friendly atmosphere.

When we arrived for our first visit (and all the subsequent ones, because we're basically regulars now), we were greeted by Justin. I'm pretty sure he sleeps on a cot in the back of the brewery because he's always there, like a dedicated beer guardian! Seriously, if there were a "Most Likely to Be Found at the Brewery" award, he'd win it paws down. Plus, he has an eidetic memory!

Justin remembers me, my humans, AND what they've tried, haven't tried, and what they like to drink. It's like he's got a little doggy database in his brain! Our top two picks are the Hazy IPA (five paws up for sure) and the Sabrovosspa (a Skint Chestnut exclusive that's so good it should come with a crown).

But wait, there's more! Not only does Justin serve up some fantastic brews, but he also has top-notch treats at the ready when you walk in. I mean, who doesn't love a place that caters to both humans and their four-legged companions? The vibe is laid back and chill, and it's not unusual for the pawrents to make friends while I'm busy charming everyone for snacks.

And if you're unsure what to try next, Justin is your go-to guy for beer recommendations. Next week, we'll be diving into Frog Rock, his first recommendation to us! Thanks for the intro Justin, because it hopped right into my favorites list!

So, if you're looking for a brewery where the beer flows like a river and the humans are as friendly as a pack of puppies, Skint Chestnut is the place to be! Cheers to good brews, great company, and the best treats around! 🍻🐾

name: 12 Bones Smokehouse & Brewing
quip: I really wanted to love this place!
review:
So, we made a quick pit stop at this brewery after a busy day, and let me tell you, I really wanted to love this place! I mean, with a name like that, I was expecting a logo that would make my non-existent tail wag off! But alas, my hopes were dashed like a squirrel running across the street.

The highlight of our visit? A super nice couple we met at the bar! She's now following me on social media, and he's a big-time philanthropist—just like yours truly! He shared some pretty cool stuff about what the community and Mills River Brewery are doing to give bikes to kids in need. It was pretty cool to see his pictures and my humans got to chat some about our passion for our veterans.

Oh, and guess what? We even met another Frenchie family! Dog-friendly? You bet! They get major props for that! But I'm not so sure how much the brewtender cared that we were there. I mean, I was giving my best puppy eyes, and all I got was a shrug. Rude!

Now, my one human ordered a fruited IPA that apparently has great global reviews. But let me tell you, she found it 'meh.' I mean, come on! If it's not making her do a happy dance, what's the point? Maybe we need to do a little more research and try again next year.

So, all in all, it was a mixed bag. Nice patrons, dog-friendly vibes, but the brews and general atmosphere not necessarily up to snuff.

name: All Sevens Brewing
quip: If you're looking for good eats, tasty brews, and a full bar, then All Sevens is the place to be!
review:
🐾🎉 Hey there, my fellow adventure seekers! It's your favorite furry foodie back at it again to tell you about another gem we discovered in Asheville! If you're looking for good eats, tasty brews, and a full bar, then All Sevens is the place to be! 🍻🍽️

Now, I have to be honest—this spot isn't totally dog-friendly, but since it was a gorgeous 65-degree December day, my humans and I decided to soak up the sunshine on the patio. And let me tell you, I took my role as the official greeter very seriously! I was racking up pets like it was my job—TONS of them! I should probably start charging for my services! 😎🐕

Our server and beertender, Amanda, was an absolute gem! She was all about the pets and had some great conversation going. I mean, who doesn't love a human who knows how to scratch the right spots? 🙌🐾

Now, my humans decided to go all out with the ultimate beer taste test—tasters of every beer on tap! Good thing I'm not drinking, because someone had to keep an eye on their shenanigans! And guess what? Their favorites actually complemented each other! One human was all about the ESB, giving it a solid 4 stars, while the other was head over heels for the American IPA. They also thought the Kolsch and Hefeweizen were worth a second taste. 🍺✨

But wait, it gets better! The humans tried the Gringo Tacos, and let me tell you, they were blown away by how tasty those little bundles of joy were. 🌮🤤

Oh, and did I mention the HUGE sticker wall? You know I had to put the Frenchies up there! We also spotted two of our favorites, Horned Owl and Buckbald, hanging out on the wall. It's always super cool to see some familiar faces out in the wild! 🦉🎉

So, if you're ever in Asheville and looking for a place to kick back, enjoy some sunshine, and indulge in tasty food and drinks, All Sevens is a must-visit! 🐾❤️

name: Angry Elk Brewing Co
quip: Finally, a place where I can take a little potty break without having to do the 'hold it' dance!
review:
🐾🎉 Alright, fur-friends, gather 'round because I've got a tail-wagging story about my latest surprise brewery adventure! This gem is right outside the Cherokee Indian Reservation, and let me tell you, it's quite the interesting spot. For us canines, it's a dream come true with a HUGE grassy area! Finally, a place where I can take a little potty break without having to do the "hold it" dance! 🌳💦

Once we strutted inside, we met Angel, our beertender extraordinaire! She was totally smitten with yours truly. She was front and center for some serious petting action, and I was living my best life. 🐶✨ Angel even traded me her sticker for mine and taped my 2023 stats on their fridge! Can you believe it?

Now, let's talk about the beer! This place is like the OG of mixing brews to create something totally new and exciting. My humans tried a bunch of different beers, but their favorites were the Sweet and Spicy—a wild combo of Jalapeño Pale Ale and Strawberry Cider—and the Snake Bite, which is a mashup of Copperhead Hazy IPA and Jalapeño Pale Ale. 🌶️🍻

So, if you find yourself in the area, maybe hitting up the casino or just passing through Whittier, do yourself a favor and stop by to see Angel! Tell her Palmer sent you. Until next time, keep those tail-less butts wagging! 🐾❤️✨

name: Archetype Brewing
quip: This spot? Pretty solid!
review:
🐾🍻 Alright, fur-friends, listen up—I've got another brewery adventure to report, and this one had me sniffing out snacks and sours like a pro!

This spot? Pretty solid! The taproom was wide open (perfect for a little casual zooming), and let me tell you—those super comfy sofas were calling my name! A prime spot for lounging or investigating, because where there are sofas, there are snacks hiding underneath. And where there are kids, well… there's at least a 75% chance someone's dropping something delicious. 🕵️‍♂️🐾

Now, my humans were winding down for the day, so they weren't going full send on the strong stuff. Their pick? False Antagonist, a Sour – Traditional Gose. And let me tell you, my human almost gave this one a 4-paw rating! It had all the juicy goodness without too much pucker—just the right level of tart and tasty.

Would I come back? Oh, absolutely! Cozy vibes, plenty of sniff-worthy corners, and prime snack-hunting opportunities? Sign me up! 🐾🍻

name: Burial Beer Co
quip: It was crazier than a dog park on a Saturday!
review:
Alright, fur-friends, gather 'round because I've got a tale about this brewery that had me wagging my tail and scratching my head at the same time! I really wanted to give this place a higher rating because my humans were raving about the beers, but it was crazier than a dog park on a Saturday! I mean, it felt like the entire town of Asheville decided to throw a party at once.

Now, let me tell you, the vibe here is super cool! They've got these vintage car frames you can hang out in, all decked out with stickers like they're the coolest cars in town. And the outdoor deck area? Massive! It's like a dog's dream playground, but on December 31, it was 70 degrees!

But here's the kicker: they only had a walk-up bar open for beers, which meant the line was longer than my list of favorite treats! My humans had to make quick decisions, so they got five different IPAs to try, but we let the beertender pick for us. So, guess what? We have no idea what we actually tried!

Now, let's talk about the canine scene—this place was a doggy paradise! I spotted FOUR English bulldogs, which is like finding a unicorn in the wild! And the kids running around were all about giving me pets! I even handed out a bunch of my stat cards like I was a celebrity!

So, while I think we need to do some additional research on this place, it's definitely a fun spot for pups and humans alike! Just be prepared for a bit of chaos and a lot of love!

name: Burning Blush Brewery
quip: "I met you last year!" — Melody, the brewer.
review:
🐾🎉 This was our second time visiting Burning Blush, and let me tell you, it was just as pawsome as last year! As soon as we walked in, Melody—the brewer and Whit's mom—exclaimed, "I met you last year!" I mean, what's better than being recognized a whole year later? I must be a celebrity in the dog world! 🌟🐶

Melody was a total sweetheart, showering me with pets and maybe sneaking me a few extra treats. 😍🍖

Burning Blush was a favorite last year, and it didn't disappoint this time either! First off, their place is super cool—like, vintage bar cool! The Lannings found this gem of a literal vintage bar and had it shipped all the way from Chicago! Plus, they've got stunning stained glass windows and a beautiful outdoor area where I could soak up the sun while my humans enjoyed their brews. ☀️🍻

Now, let's get to the good stuff—BEER! Whit seems to be all about American IPAs and lagers, and my humans were all about the Square Your Luck and Hostage to My Feelings this year. 😂🍺

But seriously, not only are they brewing some fantastic beer, but the folks at Burning Blush are just the nicest people! So, if you find yourself in the area, do yourself a favor and stop by. Cheers to good brews and even better company! 🐾🍻✨

name: Diatribe Brewing Co
quip: This is where my humans first tasted the legendary Cerebral Espionage.
review:
🐾🎉 Alright, fur-friends, let me spill the kibble about Diatribe! This is where my humans first tasted the legendary Cerebral Espionage. I can't remember what my other human tried, but they both gave the beers a solid 4 paws up! One was so good they had to try it again at the collaboration brewery. Talk about a beer that's worth double-dipping! 🍻🐶

Now, here's where it gets a little quirky: they had a random band playing with flutes, violins, and a guitar. I mean, who knew I'd be sipping brews while listening to a classical symphony? 🎶🐾

But don't worry, I still managed to snag some good pets on our way out. The other patrons were super respectful, which is always a plus when you're a Frenchie trying to soak up the attention!

They also had a great outdoor area that we didn't get to fully explore, but I can tell you this: it's definitely a place we'd return to! So if you're in the area, make sure to check out Diatribe! 🐾❤️✨

name: Eluvium Brewing Company
quip: It's like a doggy paradise!
review:
🐾🎉 Oh boy, let me tell you about our adventures at Eluvium! We loved this place so much that we practically moved in during our five-day Asheville escapade! It's not huge, but it's got both indoor and outdoor areas, and let me tell you, it's like a doggy paradise! I met all kinds of furry friends here, including one fabulous pup named Gladys! 🎤🐶

Katie, our super friendly beertender, was like a dog whisperer for humans—always right there with great recommendations! My humans taste-tested most of the menu. They did try a solid IPA, but they really went out of the box with some wild selections. The Plum Sour Lager? Oh la la, it was like a fruity party in a glass! And the Christmas Cookie Stout? Like sipping on holiday cheer in a glass! 🎄🍺

And let's not forget about the non-beer options! But let's be real, the real star of the show is the kitchen! I highly recommend trying the smash burger. Rumor has it the sweet potato fries are pretty excellent too!

So, if you're looking for a dog-friendly spot with great vibes, tasty brews, and a menu that'll make your taste buds do a happy dance, Eluvium is the place to be! 🐾❤️✨

name: Leveller Brewing Co
quip: The beer was good, the patrons were great, and the company was even better!
review:
Hey there, fur-friends! Palmer here, and let me tell you about another Asheville New Year's 2024 adventure at Leveller, a spot recommended by a fellow human at Eluvium. Apparently, this little corner of Asheville is like a brewery treasure map, with three places all within walking distance! 🍻🐶

Now, Leveller is a cozy little joint—definitely Frenchie-friendly, but sadly, no treats for us pups. But the best part? We met the cutest couple ever, Kasey and Rhett! They're just like my humans—no kids, but they love to hop from brewery to brewery like it's a game of fetch! I racked up the pets from them! 🐾❤️

Rhett was all about the stouts, while Kasey was vibing with my humans and enjoying the IPAs. The humans tried the Jake on the Puppy and the Four Horned, and both got some serious tail wags from our crew! But the Four Horned really stole the show. 🍻🍑

So, to sum it all up: the beer was good, the patrons were great, and the company was even better! Definitely worth a visit if you're in the area. 🐾✨

name: Mountain Layers Brewing Company
quip: It's got a cozy little bar area on the first floor and a rooftop patio that's perfect for soaking up the sun.
review:
Alright, fur-friends, let me tell you about this super cool spot in downtown Bryson City! It's got a cozy little bar area on the first floor and a rooftop patio that's perfect for soaking up the sun. But here's the kicker: the owners opened this place after more than 30 years of public service…

They've got this massive Honor and Remember Wall filled with all kinds of awesome patches from military veterans and first responders. I mean, we all know I'm a big supporter of our vets, so this really warmed my little canine heart!

My humans tried the Diatribe Brewing collaboration of Cerebral Espionage and the Nydee Effret IPA. They said it was a great spot to stop by and catch a view.

Now, here's the catch: it's not totally dog-friendly—only service dogs allowed. So, I had to bust out my official gear and go to work. I'd much rather be strutting my stuff in my cute harness than actually doing a job! But hey, a pup's gotta do what a pup's gotta do!

So, if you're in the area and looking for a cool spot with a heartwarming vibe and tasty brews, this is the place! Just remember to bring your service dog gear if you want to join the fun!

name: New Belgium Brewing
quip: New Belgium totally blew me away!
review:
🐾🎉 Alright, fur-friends, let me spill the kibble about my latest adventure at New Belgium! Now, I'll admit it—I'm a bit of a big brewery snob. I usually prefer those cozy, intimate spots. But let me tell you, New Belgium totally blew me away! 💥🍻

First off, let's talk about their massive outdoor turf area. It's like a doggy playground, but with LOTS of free-range kiddos running around! Thank goodness there's almost a magical forcefield keeping them from entering the "Liquid Center." The back deck overlooks the river, and the view? Absolutely pawsitively beautiful! 🌊🌞

Now, I must have made quite the impression on my first visit—we went back for seconds. On our second visit, Belle, the manager, came right up to me and knew my name! We had an awesome chat with Belle, who filled us in on how New Belgium handled the hurricane situation. They really stepped up for their employees while they were closed for almost two months fixing things up. ❤️🐾

Now, let's get to the most important part—THE BEER! They do some truly awesome IPAs! The Juice Force, Tropical Force, and Juicy Haze were all tail-wagging favorites. This was the only brewery where my humans genuinely liked every single beer they tasted! 🥳🍺

So, if you're looking for a place that's got great vibes, stunning views, and some seriously delicious brews, you've got to check out New Belgium! 🐾❤️✨

name: One World Brewing
quip: Like a hip hangout where you could sip and chill for hours.
review:
🐾🎉 Alright, fur-friends, let me tell you about this place that made me feel like I was sneaking into a secret speakeasy! I mean, you'd think it would be hard to find since it's basically in a basement, but nope! Just a little butt wiggle and a nose for adventure! 🕵️‍♂️🐶

Once inside, I could totally see how you could lose track of time. Speaking of treats, our beertender Mike was like a magician with snacks! He was Johnny-on-the-spot, ready to serve up some solid goodies. Major props to Mike for knowing how to win over a Frenchie! 🍖✨

Now, let's talk about the beer! My humans dove into a bunch of their IPAs and really loved the Citra Bomb IPA, Ashevegas, and the Aztec-nology IPA. The vibe here was super cool—like a hip hangout where you could sip and chill for hours. I can't wait to check out their other location next time we're in Asheville! 🥳🍻

So, if you're looking for a place that feels like a hidden gem and has treats for both humans and pups, this is the spot! 🐾❤️✨

name: Outsider Brewing
quip: Now, when we rolled up, I was greeted by a werewolf standing outside. I mean, what kind of place is this?
review:
🐾🎉 Hey there, party animals! Let me tell you about our last stop on our Sunday evening adventure: Outsider Brewery! Now, when we rolled up, I was greeted by a werewolf standing outside. I mean, what kind of place is this? 🌕🍻

This joint has a super cool outdoor area—some parts are covered, and others are open air. They've got this awesome "open" brewing contraption thingy where you can watch the whole brewing process unfold, from raw ingredients to the magical fermentation dance. And they even have "watch" rooms where you can peek in on the double and triple IPAs being brewed. 🍺🔍

Now, let's talk about Phil, our beertender. This guy was young but had some serious musical chops! He was jamming out to Alice in Chains and Nirvana like it was 1994. Phil was pretty chill, but he couldn't help but chuckle when I pulled off my magic act—getting loose and zooming around like a furry tornado! 🎸🐕

They keep about 10 beers on tap, and since my humans were being responsible, they decided to go all in and try most of them. Their favorites from the taste test were the Big Red IPA, a West Coast IPA, Cosmic Pale, Rice Kolsch, and the Craft Dark Lager. 🍻✨

So, if you find yourself in the area, definitely add Outsider Brewery to your must-visit list! Who knows, you might even run into a werewolf or two! 🐺🍺

name: Riverside Rhapsody Beer Company
quip: A party in my mouth. (Re: the BeBop Tango IPA.)
review:
🐾🎉 Hey, fur-friends! Let me tell you about a Sunday evening stop during our 2024 NYE Asheville trip. After a little power nap, we set out to explore some breweries that bravely weathered Hurricane Helene like champs! 🌪️🍻

Our first stop was Riverside Rhapsody. It was dark when we arrived, but I could already feel the good vibes! We definitely need to come back when the sun is shining because this place has a massive outdoor area that's just begging for some zoomies! Plus, rumor has it they have a resident BBQ company! 🍖🐶

As soon as we walked in, I knew I was in the right spot because there were two dachshunds dancing on a table like they were auditioning for "Dancing with the Stars"! Their human hooked me up with a treat that made my mouth water like never before: a HOTDOG! 🌭😍

We didn't realize it at first, but our beertender, Mike, is one of the owners! He gave us the scoop on how scary the hurricane was for the river area. We especially loved the BeBop Tango IPA—it was like a party in my mouth! 🎶🍺

So, halfway through this New Year's trip, I'm feeling like we need to plan another Asheville outing when the weather is warm! 🐾✨

name: Salt Face Mule Brewing Co
quip: This place is HUGE! It's got everything you could ever want: a brewery, a restaurant, an outdoor area, and even mini golf!
review:
🐾🎉 Hey there, my fellow snack enthusiasts! It's your favorite furry foodie here to tell you about one of our Asheville adventures in munchie land! After an intense online hunt, we decided on the Salt Faced Mule! 🐴🍻

Let me tell you, this place is HUGE! It's got everything you could ever want: a brewery, a restaurant, an outdoor area, and even mini golf! It's definitely a family-friendly spot, which is great because when a kid drops a snack – I'm a fan! And guess what? They even have COCKTAILS! 🍹💃

Now, here's the kicker: this venue isn't exactly dog-friendly, so I had to don my service dog harness and go to work. Ugh, you know how I feel about that! 😒🐕

The humans kicked things off with some fried cauliflower, a burger, and chicken tenders — there was enough to feed a small army! 🍔🐔

Now, let's get to the important stuff: the drinks! They tried the Sit a Spell Pale Ale and the Holler Atcha West Coast IPA, both of which were worth another taste. And don't even get me started on the Strawberry Jalapeño Margarita! 🌶️🍓

So, if you're ever in the area and looking for a place to munch and sip, the Salt Faced Mule is definitely worth a visit! 🐾👀🍽️

name: Sideways Farm and Brewery
quip: Where the heck did my humans bring me? Is this a brewery or a dog park for goats?
review:
🐾🎉 Hey there, fur-friends! It's your favorite Frenchie, Palmer, here to spill the beans about our New Year's trip for 2024! We kicked things off at a little brewery in the middle of nowhere called Sideways Farm and Brewery. I won't lie, when we hopped out of the car, I was like, 'Where the heck did my humans bring me? Is this a brewery or a dog park for goats?' 🐐🚗

As soon as we stepped inside, I was hit with the aroma of mulled wine that made my taste buds do a happy dance! 💃🍷 And there was this big, poofy dog who totally ignored my attempts to make friends. Rude! 🙄🐕

Now, let's talk brews! These humans of mine should've asked Dawnelle, the brewtender, for recommendations right off the bat since this was our first stop of six! They brought home a Farm Stock Ale with caramel and toffee that was out of this world! 🤤

They also enjoyed the Sake Farmhouse Ale and an IPA on tap. I'm not sure what all that means, but if it makes my humans happy, I'm all for it! 🎉🍻

So, if you find yourself wandering in the area, make sure to check out Sideways Farm and Brewery! It's a hidden gem! Cheers to new adventures and tasty brews! 🐾🍻✨

name: Sweeten Creek Brewing
quip: I was allowed in, but it felt like I was at a party where no one wanted to play with me!
review:
Alright, fur-friends, let me tell you about this brewery that had treats, beer, and BBQ! Now, I wasn't really feeling the treats—maybe because I was a little overserved at Burning Blush earlier in the day. My humans let me taste test their BBQ too and reality is… most times if the choice is BBQ or treats… I mean what would you pick? 🍖

Now, here's the kicker: I was allowed in, but it felt like I was at a party where no one wanted to play with me! I was practically giving them my best puppy eyes, and all I got was a few half-hearted pats. Rude! 😒🐶

The humans decided to do an IPA flight, and tried the Phresh Pulp, Tap Shack Session, AsheChill, and Bro, Chill. Their feelings about these brews were all over the place — it was like watching a tennis match, but with beer! 🎾🍺

So, was it worth the visit? Sure! The beer was pretty good overall, but they definitely missed the mark on some of my other doggy criteria—like, I don't know, a petting station or a treat buffet just for me? A Frenchie can dream, right? 🐾✨

name: Wedge Brewing Co
quip: The location is pretty awesome! Lots of cool shops nearby for the humans to browse.
review:
🐾🍻 Here's the scoop on our quick pit stop at Wedge Brewing! This was a drive-by sip-and-sniff mission because it was right on the river and super convenient to the other spots we were hitting. And let me tell you—the location is pretty awesome! Lots of cool shops nearby for the humans to browse, plus a great outdoor area perfect for some good ol' fashioned people-watching.

The weather? Absolute perfection. ☀️ So, of course, we snagged a spot outside and soaked up the sunshine while watching some humans get way too serious about cornhole.

One thing that makes this spot extra cool? They've got guest taps and wine, so even if you're not all about the beer, there's still something tasty to sip on! 🍷🍺 We tried the Foundy Sesh—a solid, easy-drinking brew that gets a paws-up from us!

Would we come back? For sure! Next time, we'll stick around longer, sniff out some more beers, and maybe even challenge someone to a cornhole match—Frenchie style. 🐾🍻

name: Wicked Weed Brewing
quip: It's like the doggy Disneyland of breweries!
review:
🐾🎉 Hey there, my fellow adventurers! It's your favorite furry food loving brewery hopper here to tell you about our latest escapade in downtown Asheville! We decided to swing by the original Wicked Weed, and let me tell you, this place is HUGE! I mean, it's like the doggy Disneyland of breweries! 🌟🍻

First off, the outdoor patio is massive, with tons of comfy couches and heaters—perfect for lounging in style! But the best part? It's COMPLETELY dog-friendly! I got to hang out at the bar with my humans, soaking up all the love and attention from patrons, servers, and bartenders alike. I was basically the star of the show! 🌟🐶

Now, let's talk about the humans and their beer adventures. One of them went totally off the rails with their drink choice and ordered the Double Entendre — an open-fermented Belgian dubbel with notes of plum, dates, and burnt brown sugar. It was a surprise delight! 🍷✨

And then came the food! They tried the smash burger and sweet potato fries, which were so good they practically gave it five paws up! 🍔🍟

So, if you find yourself wandering around downtown Asheville, make sure to stop by Wicked Weed! It's a must-visit for good eats, great brews, and endless pets for yours truly. 🐾❤️

name: Zebulon Artisan Ales
quip: I'm basically a celebrity now!
review:
🐾🎉 Hey, party pals! It's your favorite four-legged adventurer to spill the beans about our third stop of the morning: Zebulon! Nestled in the adorable little area of Asheville, right next to Leveller and Eluvium, this place was destined to be a blast! 🎈🍻

We decided to bring along our new buddies, Kasey and Rhett, because let's be real—beer tasting is always better with friends! As soon as we walked in, I knew we hit the jackpot! There were four canine pals already hanging out, and the beertender wasted no time in giving me some serious love. She even took my sticker and baseball card and added me to the beer fridge! I'm basically a celebrity now! 🐶✨

Now, this was the last stop before nap time, so the humans were starting to wind down. But they still managed to taste the Imperial Red Ale, and they thought it was pretty darn good! 🍺😄

So, if you're ever in Asheville, make sure to swing by Zebulon! With furry friends, friendly beertenders, and tasty brews, it's a paw-some place to hang out. 🐾❤️

name: Buckbald Brewing
quip: Great drinks, a cool vibe, and a pup-friendly atmosphere? What more could a dog ask for?
review:
Alright, folks, grab your treats and settle in because this review would be a tail wagger, if I had a tail! Writing about my pals Patrick and Matt at Buckbald is like trying to choose my favorite chew toy—impossible! Not only do they brew some killer beers that make me drool like my humans are cooking steak, but they're also two of the bestest humans a Frenchie like me could ever know. Seriously, if there were a doggy Hall of Fame, these guys would be first-ballot inductees!

Now, you all know how much I love our military and veterans (okay, it's really my humans who love them, but since they feed me, I'm claiming it too). Well, Patrick, Matt, their families, staff, and the whole community are some of the most generous folks you'll ever meet. Every year, these legends fundraise thousands of dollars for Shepherd's Men and the SHARE Military Initiative. They even brew a special beer called 22 Pale Ale to honor our military, and a portion of the proceeds goes to Shepherd's Men. Talk about brewing with a purpose! If I had thumbs, I'd give them a standing ovation!

Now, let's get to the good stuff—the beer! You can usually find anywhere from 12 to 16 beers on tap, and my humans have tried them all. One of their favorites is Mango Tango, which is a sour that's so sneaky you wouldn't even know it's sour—like a ninja in a tutu! It has a hint of habanero, and the sweet and spicy combo is so good it should come with a warning label: "May cause spontaneous happy dances!"

Then there's Buck's Frosty Balls. Yes, you read that right! But be careful, because for my pint-sized human, that 7% ABV will sneak up on you faster than I can steal a snack off the coffee table! They've also recently introduced a "hard tea" that's so tasty it'll have you feeling like you're lounging on a beach, even if you're just sitting in a parking lot!

If you find yourself in Copperhill and Patrick (the bearded wonder with fairy string) isn't around, don't fret! Look for Tina, who'll whip you up a tie-dye that's as colorful as my personality. And if you're a dawg fan, you've got to meet Jake—he's basically famous! Just ask him about his book; it's a real page-turner! And don't forget Lilly; she's always ready to dish out belly rubs like they're going out of style.

If you venture over to Murphy, make sure to say hi to Matt. He's the best! You won't miss him—he's the tall guy covered in tattoos who always has good pets and cuddles for us dogs. Seriously, if you're not getting belly rubs from him, you're doing it wrong!

So, if you're looking for great brews, amazing humans, and a place that gives back to the community, Buckbald Brewery is the place to be! Cheers to good times, great friends, and the best belly rubs around! 🍻🐾

name: Copperhill Brewing
quip: I was basically the star of the show!
review:
🐾🎉 Oh la la, fur-friends! Let me tell you about my latest adventure at Copperhill—it was a delightful surprise! We stopped by because my humans had some time to kill before visiting our pals at Buckbald. And let me tell you, this place was busier than a squirrel convention! It was a bit tricky to read the blackboards and get the beertenders' attention, but once my humans snagged some seats at the bar, it was all butt wags and good vibes! 🍻✨

Now, this place is HUGE! I'm talking about leather sofas that are so comfy, I could nap there for days. They were decked out for the holidays, and I swear I saw some stockings hanging at my eye level. I was convinced there were treats in there just waiting for a Frenchie like me to snag them! 🎄🐶

And guess what? There were tons of Frenchie lovers at Copperhill! I met a super nice Frenchie family who showered me with pets, and another couple who couldn't get enough of my adorable face. I was basically the star of the show! 🌟❤️

But let's get to the real reason you're here: the beer! My humans tried the Hazy Consequences and Valhalla. Now, let's talk about Valhalla… I mean, if you know me, you know I'm all about that military and patriotic life! It's like they brewed this beer just for me! Both IPAs were solid picks, and I can confirm they'd definitely go back for round two. 🍺

Just a little heads up: check the train schedule before you go because this place gets packed! And trust me, when the crowd rolls in, getting service can be harder than getting a treat out of my humans! So, if you're in the area, swing by Copperhill for some good brews and even better vibes! 🐾❤️✨
